entry.title: Wireshark — Network Traffic Analysis
entry.summary: Complete guide to Wireshark — capture filters, display filters, protocol dissection, TLS decryption, and real-world traffic analysis workflows for security research and CTFs.

entry.title: Windows Fundamentals — The Complete Foundation
entry.summary: Architecture, filesystem, registry, users, NTFS permissions, processes, services, PowerShell, Active Directory, event logs, and a red team lens on every layer.

entry.title: Q2 2026 Threat Landscape – Attackers Target Trust Infrastructure
entry.summary: Attackers in Q2 2026 are bypassing endpoints entirely and going straight for identity systems, VPNs, device managers, and security tools. A breakdown of actively exploited CVEs, macro trends, and what defenders should prioritise now.

entry.title: Q1 2026 Threat Landscape – Zero-Days, MDM Campaigns & AD Privilege Paths
entry.summary: Q1 2026 brought a wave of pre-authentication zero-days exploited before public disclosure, paired MDM campaigns against Ivanti EPMM, a critical Citrix NetScaler auth bypass, a Netlogon privilege path to domain control, and a public Linux kernel LPE. None of these overlap with the Q2 post.

entry.title: Threat Hunting with Sigma Rules — Writing Detections That Actually Work
entry.summary: Sigma is the vendor-neutral standard for writing detection rules that compile to any SIEM. Covers the Sigma format in depth, writing rules for real ATT&CK techniques, converting to Splunk/Elastic/QRadar queries, hunting methodologies, and building a detection pipeline that keeps pace with evolving adversaries.

entry.title: Staying Sharp — How I Keep Security Skills From Going Stale
entry.summary: Personal notes on maintaining breadth across red, blue, and cloud without burning out: the daily reading habit that actually sticks, a lab rotation schedule, CTF cadence, and an honest list of what's worth paying for versus what isn't.

entry.title: SSH — Field Guide
entry.summary: Key auth, port forwarding, SCP, SOCKS proxying, and a CTF workflow covering key cracking, pivoting, and writing your own key to a compromised box.

entry.title: SQL Injection & sqlmap – From Manual Payloads to Full Dumps
entry.summary: SQL injection is one of the most impactful web vulnerabilities. Learn manual detection and exploitation techniques, then automate the heavy lifting with sqlmap — from basic dumps to OS shell access.

entry.title: SNMP – Network Intelligence & Enumeration
entry.summary: SNMP exposes a wealth of device info — system details, interfaces, running processes, installed software. Learn to query it, brute-force community strings, and extract data for both ops and offensive use.

entry.title: SMTP — Field Guide
entry.summary: Banner grabbing, user enumeration via VRFY/RCPT, open relay testing with swaks, and a CTF workflow for turning an open relay into code execution.

entry.title: SMB — Field Guide
entry.summary: Null sessions, share enumeration, pass-the-hash, and a full CTF workflow covering smbclient, smbmap, enum4linux, CrackMapExec, Impacket, EternalBlue, and Responder.

entry.title: Reverse Engineering 101 — Reading What the Compiler Wrote
entry.summary: Reverse engineering demystified. Covers static vs dynamic analysis, essential tools, x86/x64 assembly fundamentals, reading decompiler output, patching binaries, unpacking, and a worked malware analysis walkthrough — from running sample to extracted C2 address.

entry.title: Regex for Security Analysts — The Patterns You Need and Nothing Else
entry.summary: A compact, copy-paste reference of the regex patterns that actually come up constantly in log analysis, detection rule writing, and OSINT — IPv4/IPv6, domains, hashes, base64 detection, Windows paths, and the IOC formats you'll grep for on a weekly basis.

entry.title: Red vs Blue: The Eternal Game
entry.summary: The cat-and-mouse between attackers and defenders has no finish line. A structured breakdown of every major kill-chain phase — attacker technique on the left, defender response on the right — and why purple teaming is the only way to close the loop.

entry.title: Reading Malware Reports — How to Extract Actionable Intel from a Threat Report PDF
entry.summary: A 40-page vendor threat report is not a deliverable — it's raw material. Notes on the process of turning one into four concrete artifacts: a Sigma rule, an IoC blocklist, an ATT&CK Navigator layer, and a one-paragraph management brief, with a worked example.

entry.title: Ransomware Affiliates Are Hunting Your Backups First — The Veeam RCE Chain
entry.summary: Two critical Veeam Backup & Replication CVEs (CVSS 9.4 and 9.9) let an attacker pivot from a single domain account to wiping the restore path entirely. Why backup infrastructure has become the first target in the modern ransomware kill chain, not the last.

entry.title: Purple Team Exercise — Planning and Running a Full Scenario
entry.summary: A step-by-step guide to running a structured purple team exercise: choosing a threat scenario that matches real risk, defining success criteria up front, executing the kill chain in coordination with the blue team, and converting the results into a lasting detection programme.

entry.title: Physical Security Testing — From Badge Clone to Domain Admin
entry.summary: A red team physical assessment methodology, start to finish: the pretexts that get a door opened before you touch a lock, badge cloning with a Proxmark3, mechanical bypass, and turning thirty seconds of physical access into a persistent foothold via a dropped implant.

entry.title: My Penetration Testing Methodology — How I Structure Every Engagement
entry.summary: The personal workflow I run on every engagement: the scoping questions I won't skip, how I prioritise recon, the rule I use to decide lateral movement vs. escalation, how I structure a report so findings survive the client meeting, and what three bad engagements taught me.

entry.title: OSINT — Open Source Intelligence from First Principles
entry.summary: OSINT methodology, Google dorks, Shodan, email and username enumeration, social media investigation, domain and IP recon, image geolocation, document metadata, GitHub secrets, dark web sources, and building target profiles for red team engagements.
